q: Are team sports popular in your culture?
examiner_note: Nói về tầm quan trọng của thể thao (đặc biệt là bóng đá ở Việt Nam). Sử dụng 'national identity', 'second religion', 'unite'.

band7.text: Extremely popular. Football, in particular, is like a second religion in Vietnam. Whenever our national team plays, the streets are filled with fans cheering and celebrating together. It’s a huge part of our national identity.
band7.note: Sử dụng so sánh tốt (second religion) và giải thích lý do.

band8.text: Undoubtedly. Team sports, especially football, hold a special place in the hearts of Vietnamese people. It’s not just about the game; it’s about the collective passion and national pride. When major tournaments happen, you can feel a tremendous sense of unity across the country.
band8.note: Sử dụng từ vựng mạnh (Undoubtedly, hold a special place, collective passion, tremendous, unity).

band9.text: They are deeply ingrained in our cultural fabric. Football stands out as a preeminent force that transcends social barriers, acting as a powerful catalyst for national solidarity. The fervor displayed during international fixtures is a testament to how team sports can galvanize an entire nation and foster a shared identity.
band9.note: Sử dụng ngôn ngữ học thuật và hình ảnh (ingrained in cultural fabric, preeminent, transcends, catalyst, fervor, fixtures, galvanize).

v.example: Traditions are deeply ingrained in our society.
The victory was a catalyst for national pride.
The speech helped galvanize the crowd.

advanced_vocab.example: Football is the preeminent sport in Vietnam.

similar_questions: What is the most popular sport in your country?
Why do people love football so much?
